candidate_problems.text: Identify what kind of abstract object or symmetry could make critical-line behavior necessary, rather than merely heuristically plausible, and test whether existing spectral or geometric programs already partially instantiate it.
Determine whether a non-explanatory proof of RH would count against the claim that the problem's hardness is primarily explanatory rather than computational.
Explain why analogous function-field successes have not transferred to the classical zeta setting if the missing ingredient is a bridge of the kind proposed.
